title: gossypium herbaceum var. hirsutum
intro: Gossypium herbaceum var. hirsutum, commonly known as upland cotton, is a subspecies of the cotton plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae. It is a perennial shrub native to the Americas and is known for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve wound healing. Upland cotton can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
